Name: Yellow-rumped Honeyguide
Habitat: Coniferous and mixed broadleaf forests near cliffs
Best_seen_at: Namdapha National Park (Arunachal Pradesh), Mishmi Hills
How_to_identify: Small, drably colored grey-brown bird with a diagnostic bright orange-yellow patch on the lower back and rump. Males have more prominent facial yellow. Often found near honeybee (Apis dorsata laboriosa) nests on cliffs.
Diet: Beeswax, bee larvae, and flying insects

Name: Eurasian Wryneck
Habitat: Open woodlands, orchards, and scrubby areas
Best_seen_at: Keoladeo National Park (Rajasthan), Sultanpur National Park (Haryana)
How_to_identify: Cryptic brown and grey mottled plumage resembling tree bark. Distinguished by its ability to twist its neck nearly 180 degrees. Lacks the stiff tail of true woodpeckers and often forages on the ground.

Name: Speckled Piculet
Habitat: Broadleaf forests, bamboo thickets, and secondary growth
Best_seen_at: Jim Corbett National Park (Uttarakhand), Kaziranga National Park (Assam)
How_to_identify: Tiny woodpecker with a short tail. Olive-green upperparts and creamy-white underparts heavily marked with black spots. Male has orange and yellow on the forehead; female has a plain olive forehead.
Diet: Ants, termites, and small larvae found in thin twigs or bamboo

Name: White-browed Piculet
Best_seen_at: Nameri National Park (Assam), Namdapha National Park (Arunachal Pradesh)
How_to_identify: Very small, stub-tailed bird. Rufous-orange body with olive-green wings. Features a prominent white stripe behind the eye. Males have a golden-yellow patch on the forehead.
Diet: Small insects and larvae, particularly from bamboo stems

Name: Heart-spotted Woodpecker
Habitat: Moist deciduous and evergreen forests, teak plantations
Best_seen_at: Thattekad Bird Sanctuary (Kerala), Cotigao Wildlife Sanctuary (Goa)
How_to_identify: Uniquely shaped with a large head, thin neck, and very short tail. Buff-white upperparts and wings covered in distinct black heart-shaped spots. Male has a black forehead; female has a buffy-white forehead.

Name: Brown-capped Pygmy Woodpecker
Habitat: Open forests, riverine woodlands, and urban gardens
Best_seen_at: Pench Tiger Reserve (Madhya Pradesh), Bandipur National Park (Karnataka)
How_to_identify: A tiny woodpecker with a brown crown and white-barred blackish-brown upperparts. Pale underparts with faint brown streaks. Male has a very small, often hidden red speck on the side of the crown.

Name: Grey-capped Pygmy Woodpecker
Habitat: Broadleaf forests, hilly regions, and sub-Himalayan tracts
How_to_identify: Distinguished from Brown-capped by its grey crown and darker eye-stripe. Upperparts are black with prominent white barring. Male has small red streaks on the sides of the nape.
Diet: Insects, larvae, and occasionally pulp of fruits

Name: Brown-fronted Woodpecker
Habitat: Subtropical pine and montane oak forests
Best_seen_at: Chopta (Uttarakhand), Great Himalayan National Park (Himachal Pradesh)
How_to_identify: Medium-small woodpecker with a brown forehead. Male has a yellow crown and red nape; female has a completely yellow crown without red. Underparts are heavily streaked with black.

Name: Yellow-crowned Woodpecker
Habitat: Arid scrub, dry deciduous forests, and agricultural edges
Best_seen_at: Desert National Park (Rajasthan), Sultanpur National Park (Haryana)
How_to_identify: Golden-yellow crown and a small red patch on the belly. Upperparts are black with white spotting. Males have a red patch on the back of the crown which females lack.
Diet: Insects, particularly grubs and ants, and nectar

Name: Crimson-naped Woodpecker
Habitat: Subtropical and tropical moist montane forests
Best_seen_at: Eaglenest Wildlife Sanctuary (Arunachal Pradesh), Neora Valley (West Bengal)
How_to_identify: Small woodpecker with black upperparts barred with white. Features a prominent red patch on the nape and a red wash on the breast and vent. Throat is white with black moustachial stripes.
Diet: Insects and larvae extracted from mossy branches
